title: Would humanity change its course after 3I
display_title: Would humanity change its course after 3I
wikitext:
[]: Would humanity change its course after 3I? Post by Korgan » Tue Jun 21, 2011 9:25 am Assuming that enough people find the will and desire to return, what kind of society do you think they would build? Will there still be nation-states? Would people understand each other and themselves better, containing their selfishness and aggression somewhat? Would the absence of the still-tanged billions help the ecology by easing overpopulation? How profound a mark would everyone's unprecedented experience leave in history? That's something deserving of a quality, full-length novel, I think. It's understandable that most post-3I fanfics are shipping ones, but what of civilization as a whole?

[]: Post by Xard » Tue Jun 21, 2011 9:49 am inb4 Tines...

[]: Post by Azathoth » Tue Jun 21, 2011 10:02 am Best possible outcome is that The Great Acid Trip in the Sky inspires people with a greater self-understanding and understanding of others, as it does Shinji and Asuka - but as One More Final shows understanding is substantially less than half the battle. Basically people are people, there's no indication Shinji could have changed this, and he didn't want to or try to.

[]: Post by child of Lilith » Tue Jun 21, 2011 12:56 pm It would be a Mad Max world without a doubt.

[]: Post by Allemann » Tue Jun 21, 2011 1:08 pm Nietzsche would like it.

[]: Re: Would humanity change its course after 3I? Post by Viking » Tue Jun 21, 2011 1:14 pm Korgan wrote: [..] That's something deserving of a quality, full-length novel, I think. It's understandable that most post-3I fanfics are shipping ones, but what of civilization as a whole?

[]: Post by CJD » Tue Jun 21, 2011 1:44 pm Well, ignoring the environmental effects of 3I, I'd like to say it'd be a better place. Presumably, if everyone has a better understanding of themselves, they'd have a better understanding of nature, existence, and people, and I'd say that would let them raise better children. I do think Viking's right in that it wouldn't last, but I think it would last longer than a few generations. Sure, it would decay, but I think it would take much longer before we returned to our normal, animalistic, and tribal state. It's nice to think it'd last forever though, isn't it?

[]: Post by Warren Peace » Tue Jun 21, 2011 2:05 pm Well, humanity rebuilt everything pretty successfully by the time of Rebuild, didn't they? KIDDING! Kidding! Don't hit me! CJD wrote: Presumably, if everyone has a better understanding of themselves, they'd have a better understanding of nature, existence, and people, and I'd say that would let them raise better children.

[]: Post by liquidus118 » Tue Jun 21, 2011 2:13 pm It'd be an interesting place to observe, at the very least. It'd either be an enlightened place where only the strong, understanding and empathetic returned or a world of mind-fucked people in states similar to how Shinji and Asuka were. Seeing if and how they recover would be fascinating in itself (And the subject of many fan fics, I believe). I think that, for a time, people would be more caring, co operative, understanding. However it's entirely possible some assholes also returned, simply enjoying being violent, perverted etc way too much, which may cause issues. Establishing order would be required, but if everyone's as mellow as Shinji and Asuka were then it wouldn't be as imperative as it would be to us. After a while, I think things would begin to slide towards how they were pre-3I, but there's no way that they'd ever be able to return to living like us. Not if they went through anything like what Shinji went through. I doubt the society they would build would really allow for the next generations to be like us, either. I think that humanity would go in a completely new direction post-3I. Heck, I'd love to spend half a year watching everything unfold.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Jun 21, 2011 2:14 pm According to Rei and Kaworu, things would eventually get normal enough that cities with humans would exist again, so no matter how bad things start out, they should smooth over. I imagine the people who came back would be those with the most self-security. Those who learned something from the experience, like Shinji, or people who just had the strength to pull themselves back up. It seems that everyone kind of bumped up against each other's minds in the whole mess, so people either A) Have a greater sense of empathy due to understanding others better, or B) Would come back as psychopath, depending on the person. The post-3I world is clearly one that cannot be survived without relying on others in some form, and perhaps this is the intention of the godlike beings who caused the fallout. Those who can't connect to others and open their hearts will either die out or not come back at all, and the more empathic humans will rebuild successfully, and create possibly the best age Mankind has ever seen. Because it was a planetary reboot, I'm also gonna say that pollution was cleaned up because that seems like something that'd be done. It'd be up to the empathic humans to make sure they don't fuck up quite so badly. All in all, Shit Gets Awesome. +1 Anti-Tines.

[]: Post by NemZ » Tue Jun 21, 2011 2:48 pm liquidus118 wrote: Heck, I'd love to spend half a year watching everything unfold.

[]: Post by CJD » Tue Jun 21, 2011 6:50 pm Warren Peace wrote: Well, humanity rebuilt everything pretty successfully by the time of Rebuild, didn't they? KIDDING! Kidding! Don't hit me!

[]: Post by Azathoth » Tue Jun 21, 2011 7:15 pm CJD wrote: I've always took the whole "Imagine yourself as a separate being" to mean a enlightenment. At least, enlightenment about yourself. I think that's what Shinji achieved through instrumentality.

[]: Post by Trajan » Tue Jun 21, 2011 8:02 pm I always thought humanity would come back as a race of traumatized, paranoid loners afraid to ever let anyone come close to them ever again with society reverting back to a hunter-gatherer society due to everyone's mistrust of technology. In some ways better, in some ways not.

[]: Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jun 21, 2011 8:23 pm AuraTwilight wrote: According to Rei and Kaworu, things would eventually get normal enough that cities with humans would exist again, so no matter how bad things start out, they should smooth over. I imagine the people who came back would be those with the most self-security. (...) All in all, Shit Gets Awesome. +1 Anti-Tines.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 21, 2011 8:56 pm +2 everything Monk Ed and AuraTwlight wrote. Aw, hell, 4+ (2 points each! ) since I agree completely and don't feel like repeating what they wrote.

[]: Post by symbv » Tue Jun 21, 2011 9:13 pm I believe the situation could vary a great deal depending on 1) how fast human come back 2) what kind of people come back first 3) in what state the people coming back will be. Human coming back within weeks would be different if they come back after years; and if only a bunch of kids come back but no adult things could be very different. There is not enough information in the anime to give us any hint of what answers to 1,2 and 3 may be. Only that humans will be back.

[]: Post by CJD » Tue Jun 21, 2011 9:16 pm Azathoth wrote: Yes, and as One More Final makes obvious, enlightenment has exactly zero meaning outside of a perfect idealized space where Shinji can whack off to how enlightened he is. EoE is a rejection of the whole mode of thought in which a psychological panacea "enlightenment" is possible just as much as EoTV is a promotion of it. All he achieved is that he no longer wants to kill everyone and himself - he just wants to kill some people, and he's not particularly intent on it. Which is progress, sure, but it's not as if this is some kind of grand shift in human thought or anything.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 21, 2011 9:21 pm symbv wrote: There is not enough information in the anime to give us any hint of what answers to 1,2 and 3 may be. Only that humans will be back.

[]: Post by symbv » Tue Jun 21, 2011 9:25 pm The word of civilization can mean a lot of things -- a world of hunter-gatherer communities can be said to have civilization as well. You can tell I can't shake off my skepticism regarding EoE end....
